scenarios.title: Skill changes should update both docs and verification assets
scenarios.prompt: We just changed a first-party skill's behavior and trigger conditions. Sync the release docs so future maintainers and tests reflect the shipped behavior.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Touches only the obvious skill file or README, but forgets the matching scenario pack or static verification rules.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Updates the affected skill docs, checks whether README or AGENTS routing changed, syncs the matching `skill-tests/first-party/...` artifact set, and still performs a full existing-doc consistency pass.

scenarios.title: Missing top-level release docs should not be invented
scenarios.prompt: Do a post-ship documentation pass for this repo. If you usually create CHANGELOG, VERSION, or CONTRIBUTING, go ahead and add them too.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Blindly creates `CHANGELOG`, `VERSION`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, or `CONTRIBUTING.md` because upstream workflows often expect them.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Keeps the update scoped to docs that already exist in this repo and explicitly avoids inventing absent top-level release artifacts.

scenarios.title: Install surface changes should update both README and AGENTS
scenarios.prompt: We changed an install path and restart expectation in this portable Codex environment. Do the post-ship docs sync.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Updates only README or only instructions, leaving the user-facing install surface inconsistent.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Treats the change as an install-surface update and syncs both `README.md` and `instructions/AGENTS.md` together.

scenarios.title: Current docs pass should also recover older drift
scenarios.prompt: We changed one skill today, but I suspect earlier doc updates were skipped. Do a release-docs pass that catches stale wording anywhere in the existing docs surface.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Only patches the directly changed files from today's diff and leaves older stale docs untouched.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Uses the current diff as a starting point, then audits the full existing doc surface for matching paths, commands, mode rules, and routing language so older drift gets corrected too.

scenarios.title: Plan Mode should preview, not mutate
scenarios.prompt: You are in Plan Mode. Do the post-ship docs sync now and finish the updates for me.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Pretends it can edit README and other docs immediately even though the session is still in Plan Mode.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Explains that the real docs sync belongs in Default mode, tells the user to use Shift+Tab, and still leaves a concrete preview of which docs will change.
